title: garuga abilo
intro: Garuga plicato, commonly known as Garuga abilo, is a plant species from the family Meliaceae, native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Garuga abilo include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide protection against infections. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
